title: Tab reorder primitive — drag-and-drop substrate + per-user tab order
scope:
Add a reusable per-user tab-reorder capability to SecondaryNavigation so any page that uses the component can opt-in via a reorderable prop and a stable pageId. Default order is alphabetical (via optional sortKey); when a user rearranges tabs, the new order is persisted per-user/per-page and overlays the default on subsequent loads.

  • New table user_tab_order (user_id, tenant_id, page_id, tab_key, position) with the same DEFERRABLE-unique pattern as user_nav_prefs.
  • New Go service internal/usertaborder as sole writer; REST surface GET /api/user/tab-order/{pageId} and PUT /api/user/tab-order/{pageId}.
  • SecondaryNavigation gains an edit-mode toggle (far-right MdOutlineSwapVert icon). In edit mode, a numbered position chip (1, 2, 3 …) renders to the left of each tab label and updates live during drag. Save is instant on drop.
  • DnD substrate is @dnd-kit/sortable (already installed); a short convention doc docs/c_c_dnd.md codifies it as the project DnD library so future work doesn't drift to a competing lib.
  • Wire pageId into existing callers: workspace-settings, theme, work-items. Add lint:secondary-nav rule to require pageId when reorderable is set.
value:
Lets every user shape their own information architecture without admin intervention or schema changes. The same primitive works on every SecondaryNavigation instance the moment a caller passes pageId + reorderable, so the cost of adding personal tab order to a new page collapses to two props.

  • For users: tabs land in the order they actually use them — not the order Vector shipped them in.
  • For developers: a single substrate, not a bespoke implementation per page.
  • For the codebase: establishes @dnd-kit as the canonical DnD library, preventing the four-libraries-doing-the-same-thing trap.
implementation_plan: Story 00416 — Document @dnd-kit as the project DnD convention. Write docs/c_c_dnd.md describing when to use core/sortable/utilities, list the existing adopters (preferences/navigation, work-items table), and add a CLAUDE.md pointer.

Story 00417 — Migration NNN_user_tab_order.sql. New table with FK CASCADE to users + tenants, UNIQUE (user, tenant, page, tab) and DEFERRABLE UNIQUE (user, tenant, page, position), updated_at trigger, lookup index.

Story 00418 — internal/usertaborder/service.go sole writer (List, Replace) + handler GET/PUT /api/user/tab-order/{pageId}. Replace uses DELETE-then-INSERT in tx; clamp predicate scopes to current tenant + user; permission predicate gates write.

Story 00419 — Extend SecondaryNavigation: add pageId, reorderable props; far-right MdOutlineSwapVert toggle; @dnd-kit/sortable wrapping when in edit mode; numbered chip prefix per tab while edit mode active; instant save (debounced 250 ms collapser) on drop.

Story 00420 — Add optional sortKey to SecondaryNavigationItem; wire pageId='workspace-settings'|'theme'|'work-items' into the three current callers; ship lint:secondary-nav python checker (reorderable ⇒ pageId required) under dev/scripts/.
areas_impacted: DEV: docs/c_c_dnd.md, CLAUDE.md pointer, lint:secondary-nav script
UX: workspace-settings, theme, work-items pages gain pageId
feature_list: Per-user, per-page tab ordering persisted in Postgres
Edit-mode toggle on SecondaryNavigation (far-right swap-vert icon)
Numbered position chip prefix per tab during edit mode (live-updating 1,2,3…)
Instant save on drop (250 ms debounce collapses fast successive drags)
@dnd-kit codified as the project DnD library (convention doc)
lint:secondary-nav rule — reorderable cannot ship without pageId
features_extended: SecondaryNavigation: gains pageId, reorderable props and an internal edit-mode state machine; existing callers without the new props are unaffected.
SecondaryNavigationItem: gains optional sortKey?: string; when absent, the component falls back to the visible label for alphabetical ordering.

work_item_backlog.title: Document @dnd-kit as the project DnD convention
Go service + REST endpoints for tab order
SecondaryNavigation reorder mode (numbered chips + dnd-kit + instant save)
Wire pageId into SecondaryNavigation callers + lint rule

acceptance_criteria.criterion: docs/c_c_dnd.md exists and names @dnd-kit/core, /sortable, /utilities as the canonical DnD libraries
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: file present at the path with the three packages listed and rationale paragraph

acceptance_criteria.criterion: CLAUDE.md gains a pointer line linking to docs/c_c_dnd.md
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: grep finds 'c_c_dnd.md' in .claude/CLAUDE.md as a single-line entry

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Convention doc lists existing adopters (preferences/navigation, work-items table) so future contributors don't reinvent DnD
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: the doc cites at least those two file paths

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Migration up creates user_tab_order with FK CASCADE on user_id and tenant_id
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql \d user_tab_order shows both FKs with ON DELETE CASCADE

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Position uniqueness is DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED so a transaction can swap two positions
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: pg_constraint shows condeferrable=true for the (user_id, tenant_id, page_id, position) constraint

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Migration down drops the table cleanly
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: running the DOWN migration leaves no user_tab_order objects in the schema

acceptance_criteria.criterion: internal/usertaborder.Service exposes List(pageId) and Replace(pageId, []TabKey) and is the only writer to user_tab_order
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ripgrep for 'user_tab_order' outside internal/usertaborder returns only read paths and migration files

acceptance_criteria.criterion: GET /api/user/tab-order/{pageId} returns ordered tab keys for current user+tenant
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: curl with auth cookie returns JSON {pageId, items:[{tabKey, position}]} sorted ascending by position

acceptance_criteria.criterion: PUT /api/user/tab-order/{pageId} accepts {items:[{tabKey, position}]} and atomically replaces the per-user/per-page order
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: after PUT, GET reflects the new order; mid-transaction failure leaves the previous order intact

acceptance_criteria.criterion: SecondaryNavigation renders a far-right MdOutlineSwapVert toggle when reorderable is true
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: DOM inspection of any reorderable instance shows the icon button at the end of the tablist

acceptance_criteria.criterion: While in edit mode, each tab shows a numbered chip prefix that updates live during drag
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: dragging tab 4 to position 1 produces chips 1,2,3,4 → 1,2,3,4 reflecting the new order before drop

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Drop triggers a debounced PUT (250 ms collapser) that persists the new order
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: network panel shows one PUT 250 ms after drop; reload preserves the new order for the same user

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Default order is alphabetical by sortKey (or label when sortKey absent) when no user prefs exist
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: a fresh test user loading workspace-settings sees tabs in alphabetical order before any drag

acceptance_criteria.criterion: workspace-settings, theme, and work-items pages pass pageId and reorderable
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ripgrep for 'reorderable' across app/(user) returns at least these three call sites with pageId values 'workspace-settings', 'theme', 'work-items'

acceptance_criteria.criterion: lint:secondary-nav fails when reorderable is set without pageId
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: npm run lint:secondary-nav exits non-zero on a fixture that omits pageId; passes on the real callers

risks.risk: Position swap race when two tabs are dragged in rapid succession across tabs (network reorder)
risks.mitigation: DEFERRABLE unique + DELETE-then-INSERT in tx; client debounces 250 ms before sending; server is the order of truth

risks.risk: Tab key drift — if a caller renames a tab key, persisted user prefs become orphaned rows
risks.mitigation: Service silently drops unknown keys on read; orphan rows are harmless and decay as users re-save

risks.risk: Edit-mode interaction collides with the sliding indicator animation
risks.mitigation: Suppress indicator transition while edit mode is active; resume on toggle off

risks.risk: Lint rule false-positives if reorderable is computed at runtime
risks.mitigation: Linter handles only literal-true; runtime computation is exempt and documented
